summary: I compiled the three review critics' verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. Rather than trusting the prior iteration's claims, I independently verified each critic's state against the current code: the requirements-conformance critic passes on all 50 requirements (R1–R50); the code-quality critic's `needs_work` verdict rested on four performance/consistency findings with no correctness or security defects, and I confirmed all four are genuinely fixed in the current code (`broadcast_card.rb`'s `dashboard:` flag, per-source DB `LIMIT`s in `recent_activity.rb`, sweep's union-of-stuck-and-unstuck broadcast, and manager_tick's single guarded broadcast); and the guide-alignment critic's `needs_work` verdict rests on five minor UI/style-guide items that I confirmed are still open. I also corrected one inaccuracy from the previous report — the 213-test/rubocop-clean evidence comes from the Build phase `test-critic` (verdict pass, with the documented chromedriver/Chrome mismatch blocking only the two system tests), not a re-run this iteration. The report covers what was asked, what was built, evidence of conformance, resolved findings, the five open minor findings, and a merge recommendation flagging UI-5 (fleet polling vs streams) as needing a manager decision.
